string.text: Enable or disable the MAI mod entirely. When disabled, NPCs will ignore anomalies completely.
Enable debug logging to console. Shows detection rolls, damage events, and death attribution.
Enable verbose debug logging. Shows update loop info, cache rebuilds, restriction changes. Very spammy!
Show all cached anomalies on PDA map as markers. Useful for debugging pathfinding. Toggle off to remove markers.
Remove leftover anomaly markers from previous sessions. Enable, apply, then disable. One-time cleanup action.
How far NPCs can detect anomalies. Higher = earlier detection but more pathfinding restrictions. Default: 30m
Delay after surge/psy-storm before rebuilding anomaly cache. Needed for Arrival mod compatibility. Default: 2.5s
NPCs beyond this distance from player are protected from anomaly damage. Prevents distant NPC deaths. Default: 300m
If enabled, all NPCs avoid anomalies regardless of distance. If disabled, only NPCs within effect radius get restrictions.
How long an NPC remembers a detected anomaly after leaving the area. Remembered anomalies bypass the miss roll, so avoidance stays reliable. Set to 0 to disable memory entirely. Default: 30s
Apply damage reduction based on NPC outfit. Reads protection values from outfit configs (burn, shock, chemical, etc).
NPCs with 0% base miss chance become completely immune to anomaly damage. Safety net for veterans.
Prevent anomalies from activating on dead bodies. Stops ragdoll physics glitches and improves performance.
Prevent NPCs from walking into anomaly fields to loot corpses. Stops chain-deaths where stalkers follow each other into anomalies. Default: ON
Miss chance multiplier when NPC is standing still. Lower = more careful observation. Default: 0.3x
Miss chance multiplier when NPC is walking. Default: 0.6x
Miss chance multiplier when NPC is running. Higher = more likely to miss anomalies. Default: 1.2x
Miss chance multiplier when NPC is in combat. Distracted NPCs are much more likely to run into anomalies. Default: 3.0x
Base miss chance for Novice stalkers. 100% = always might walk into anomalies. Default: 100%
Base miss chance for Trainee stalkers. Default: 75%
Base miss chance for Experienced stalkers. Default: 50%
Base miss chance for Professional stalkers. Default: 25%
Base miss chance for Veteran stalkers. 0% = never walks into anomalies. Default: 0%
Base miss chance for Expert stalkers. Default: 0%
Base miss chance for Master stalkers. Default: 0%
Base miss chance for Legend stalkers. Default: 0%
Allow NPCs to use engine bolt-throwing for anomaly detection in dense fields. Disable to use script restrictions only (no bolts thrown). Default: ON

Maximum seconds an NPC stays in bolt-throwing mode per encounter. Caps bolt accumulation and prevents NPCs from looping in Smart Bolts indefinitely. NPC switches to script restrictions until fully clear of anomalies, then Smart Bolts re-arms. Default: 5s
Minimum nearby anomalies before NPCs use engine bolt-throwing to probe the field. In combat, bolt-throwing is always disabled. Default: 3
Seconds an NPC can have path restrictions continuously active before stuck recovery triggers. Clears restrictions and gives a 6s grace period to move freely. Default: 8

After 3 consecutive stuck timeouts, suspend all MAI restrictions for 20s so the NPC can cross the field. Engine-native anomaly damage still applies during this window. Disable to cap recovery at the 15s cooldown instead. Default: ON
Sin (greh) faction NPCs are immune to anomaly damage. Default: ON
Sin (greh) faction NPCs do not get pathfinding restrictions to avoid anomalies. Default: ON
Monolith faction NPCs are immune to anomaly damage (protected by faith). Default: ON
Monolith faction NPCs do not get pathfinding restrictions to avoid anomalies. Default: OFF
